title: Is Marinara-sauce Gluten Free? | BiteRight
description: Public gluten safety analysis for Marinara-sauce. See major risks, safer alternatives, and what to ask before ordering.
heading: Is Marinara-sauce gluten free?
intro: This public analysis report explains the biggest gluten risks in Marinara-sauce and how to order more safely.

verdict.summary: While traditional marinara sauce is often made from gluten-free ingredients, cross-contamination during preparation or the use of gluten-containing additives can pose a risk for those with coeliac disease. It's important to verify the ingredients and preparation methods used.

disclaimer: This guidance is informational only. Always verify ingredients and preparation with the restaurant.

sections.title: Quick answer
sections.body: Marinara-sauce can vary by recipe, ingredients, and cross-contact controls in the kitchen.

sections.title: Common gluten risks
sections.body: Watch for hidden sources like soy sauce, malt flavoring, marinades, thickeners, and shared fryers.

sections.title: How BiteRight helps
sections.body: Scan menus and ingredient labels with BiteRight to get a localized gluten-risk breakdown in seconds.

risk: Wheat flour (used as a thickener in some recipes)
Pre-packaged marinara sauce with gluten-containing additives

waiter_script.preview: Can you confirm if the marinara sauce is made without gluten-containing ingredients or cross-contamination?

faq.question: Can BiteRight confirm if Marinara-sauce is gluten free?
faq.answer: BiteRight highlights likely gluten risks based on ingredients and preparation. Always confirm with the kitchen if you have coeliac disease.

faq.question: What should I ask a restaurant?
faq.answer: Ask about shared equipment, sauces, marinades, and whether the kitchen has a dedicated gluten-free prep area.
